nodes.name: types.rs

nodes.name: SparsityProfile

nodes.name: ComputeBudget
nodes.summary: Iteration/time/tolerance budget governing solver termination and lane selection.

nodes.name: SolverResult
nodes.summary: Generic solver output containing solution vector, convergence info, and algorithm chosen.

nodes.name: validate_output
nodes.summary: Sanity checks on SolverResult ensuring finite solution and reasonable convergence stats.

nodes.summary: Forward/backward push and random-walk PPR tests on synthetic graphs (star, complete, cycle).

nodes.name: test_router.rs
nodes.summary: Router selection tests verifying Neumann/CG/push picks and fallback chain behavior.

nodes.name: test_validation.rs
nodes.summary: Validation rejection tests for NaN, Inf, dimension mismatch, bad CSR, and oversized inputs.

layers.name: LLM Extras
layers.description: CLI, WASM bindings, retrieval-diffusion, and sparse-attention extensions to the ruvllm runtime.

layers.name: Decompiler
layers.description: Binary/IR decompilation engine with its WASM-targeted companion crate.

layers.name: Solver
layers.description: Constraint/optimization solver core plus Node.js and WASM target bindings.

layers.name: Temporal Tensor
layers.description: Time-aware tensor primitives and the matching WASM build.

layers.name: Graph Transformer
layers.description: Graph-attention transformer engine exposed natively, via Node, and via WASM.

tour.title: Cluster Overview: 14 Crates, 5 Subsystems
tour.description: Slice 10d collects RuVector's specialized AI/ML extension crates into five tightly-themed layers: LLM extras (CLI + WASM + retrieval/diffusion + sparse attention), a binary decompiler, a sublinear solver, a temporal-tensor compression store, and a graph transformer. Each subsystem ships a native Rust core and most have Node.js (napi) and/or WASM language bindings, giving the same engines a triple deployment target: server, browser, and Node.

tour.title: LLM Extras: CLI, WASM, Retrieval-Diffusion, Sparse Attention
tour.description: The LLM layer surrounds RuVector's core inference with four companions. ruvllm-cli is the user-facing binary that dispatches into chat/serve/quantize/benchmark/download subcommands and centralizes model metadata in models.rs. ruvllm-wasm exposes IntelligentLLMWasm to browsers/Node; ruvllm_retrieval_diffusion implements a Retriever/Diffuser pair for RAG-style sampling; ruvllm_sparse_attention provides a Tensor3 primitive and an F32Ext math trait that the sparse kernels build on. Together they form the 'LLM application surface' on top of the engine.

tour.title: Decompiler: Binary IR to Reconstructed Code
tour.description: ruvector-decompiler reverses compiled binaries back into structured IR. lib.rs declares the public module surface; types.rs (the cluster's #2 fan-in node at 13 incoming edges) defines the core IR data structures shared everywhere; inferrer.rs runs the type/shape inference passes, graph.rs holds the call/control-flow graph, training.rs handles the ML-assisted reconstruction model, and ruvector-decompiler-wasm exposes the engine to JS sandboxes for in-browser binary analysis.

tour.title: Solver: Sublinear Constraint Engine + Bindings
tour.description: ruvector-solver is the most depended-upon crate in the slice. types.rs (fan-in 25 — the single most-imported file) defines CsrMatrix sparse storage and the Algorithm enum used everywhere; error.rs (fan-in 11) and traits.rs (fan-in 9, three core traits) lock down the contract every solver backend implements. The solver ships through two binding crates: ruvector-solver-node wraps it as a napi NapiSolver for Node.js, while ruvector-solver-wasm exposes JsSolver and an analyze_sparsity helper to browsers.

tour.title: Temporal Tensor: Quantized, Delta-Encoded Tiered Storage
tour.description: ruvector-temporal-tensor is a purpose-built storage stack for time-varying tensor data. The pipeline flows bitpack -> quantizer (per-group scaled integer with variable bit-width) -> delta (sparse delta chains across time) -> segment (self-describing binary format) -> compressor (high-level facade) -> store (TieredStore with BlockKey/Tier/BlockIO) -> tiering (exponential-decay scoring and cold-tier migration). The lib.rs crate root re-exports all seven modules so callers see a single flat API.

tour.title: Graph Transformer Core: Five Attention Paradigms in One Engine
tour.description: ruvector-graph-transformer composes five distinct attention/message-passing paradigms behind one GraphTransformer entry point in lib.rs. sublinear_attention.rs uses LSH+PPR sampling to escape O(n^2); physics.rs adds Hamiltonian, gauge-equivariant, Lagrangian, and conservative-PDE attentions; manifold.rs operates on product Riemannian manifolds with curvature-adaptive routing and a Riemannian Adam optimizer; biological.rs implements spiking/dendritic/Hebbian/STDP primitives; verified_training.rs gates every training step on invariants with rollback strategies. config.rs and error.rs (fan-in 9 and 10) bind the subsystems with shared configuration and a unified error enum.

tour.title: Graph Transformer Bindings: Node + WASM Deployment
tour.description: The graph transformer ships to non-Rust runtimes through two thin binding crates. ruvector-graph-transformer-node wraps the engine as a napi GraphTransformer for Node.js servers, and ruvector-graph-transformer-wasm exposes a JsGraphTransformer to wasm-bindgen consumers (browser, edge workers, Deno). Both wrap the same underlying Rust GraphTransformer composition from Step 6 — the same composition of five attention paradigms — proving the slice's recurring 'one Rust core, three language targets' deployment shape.
